title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: If you're going to go out for a walk you'll be able to enjoy the movie.

title: will / shall
examples: If you're going to go out for a walk you'll be able to enjoy the movie.
When you make a promise you'll be able to learn a lot of these three steps.
You'll be able to make a promise until September.
For those who bought it I'll give you the cake.
For those who bought the last month I picked 10 of them so I'll give you a gift for the first year of Kakao service.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: Chuseok, please buy it before you go.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: 근데 원어민들은 크게 넘어졌다 할 때 It shit을 사용합니다 일종의 슬랭이죠 일상생활에서 정말 많이 사용하죠 You can say he just ate shit Or I almost ate shit 입도 알고 shit도 알지만 그냥 들었을 때는 무슨 말인지 전혀 모를 것 같아 엄청 크게 넘어졌어 D-H-E-T Number two, spill the tea Oh my god, there was so much drama today What happened?
You can crash on the couch if you want Number four, big back 음, gosh, I'm such a big back big도 알죠?
You can do this by mimicking me or changing the context slightly so that it fits your own situations.
Next time you go for a walk you can say something simple like I'm taking a stroll or do you want to take a stroll?
Maybe you can try saying that before leaving your house.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: 근데 두 개 붙이면 무슨 말인지 모르겠네 Actually, it's close to that big back because if you have a big back It might mean that you're a larger person big back is kind of genzy slang these days it means eating a lot or being overweight It's kind of a way of calling someone fat 나 뿜 마리네 so don't say it to other people but a lot of people use it jokingly about themselves 아 나 오늘 완전 대진들?
Also the better you get at listening it might take less and less time that you have to listen.

So the first vlog you might have to listen 20 times to understand it and then later if you're watching a different vlog maybe your listening has improved and you only have to listen 3 times or 5 times and you start catching on more quickly.

title: have to / must
examples: Also the better you get at listening it might take less and less time that you have to listen.

So the first vlog you might have to listen 20 times to understand it and then later if you're watching a different vlog maybe your listening has improved and you only have to listen 3 times or 5 times and you start catching on more quickly.
Finally, you must apply what you've learned to your own life.

title: had better / it's time
examples: Okay, it's time to hit the road.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I think it would be nice if you study.
I think it would be nice if you study.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: 근데 두 개 붙이면 무슨 말인지 모르겠네 Actually, it's close to that big back because if you have a big back It might mean that you're a larger person big back is kind of genzy slang these days it means eating a lot or being overweight It's kind of a way of calling someone fat 나 뿜 마리네 so don't say it to other people but a lot of people use it jokingly about themselves 아 나 오늘 완전 대진들?
